title: blaeria mannii
intro: Blaeria mannii, commonly known as Mountain Heath, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The therapeutic actions of Blaeria mannii include its ability to treat various conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues, while also exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. The bioactive constituents of Blaeria mannii include iridoids, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Blaeria mannii can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. Consuming the plant can lead to nausea, diarrhea, and vomiting in some individuals. In severe cases, improper use of Blaeria mannii can cause anaphylaxis, a life-threatening allergic reaction. Excessive or prolonged use can also lead to kidney or liver damage, requiring immediate medical attention.
